task_completion.description: Verify that the #REF! error (originally in row 6 column B) or any other visible formula errors have been fixed and replaced with valid calculated values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the spreadsheet cells, particularly around row 6 column B. There should be no '#REF!' or other error codes visible; they should be replaced by valid numbers.

task_completion.description: Verify that descriptive labels have been added to clarify the cryptic abbreviations (SR, GEN, STD, COMP, GP, OH, NP, BEP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the row or column headers. The cryptic abbreviations should be replaced by or accompanied by clear, descriptive text (e.g., 'Gross Profit' for GP, 'Net Profit' for NP, 'Break Even Point' for BEP, 'Student', 'General', etc.).

task_completion.description: Verify that cell comments have been added to explain the formulas or data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for small colored triangles in the corners of cells (the standard indicator for cell comments in spreadsheet software like LibreOffice Calc), or an open comment popup box displaying explanatory text.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 'Documentation' sheet has been created and contains explanations of the workbook's logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the sheet tabs at the bottom of the spreadsheet window. There must be a tab named 'Documentation'. If this tab is active in the screenshot, it should contain text explaining the spreadsheet's formulas and abbreviations.

task_completion.description: Verify that the updated file is saved as 'theater_revenue_documented.xlsx' in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the spreadsheet application or a file explorer window. The filename should clearly be 'theater_revenue_documented.xlsx' and the path should indicate it is in '/home/ga/Documents/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with a spreadsheet GUI application (like LibreOffice Calc) to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the spreadsheet application open with the data loaded, rather than just terminal windows executing Python/bash scripts to manipulate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the documentation sheet contains actual explanations of the logic, not just a copy-paste of the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the Documentation sheet is visible, the text should describe the specific revenue formulas and abbreviations, not just repeat the task instructions.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just rename the file without making the required internal changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the open 'theater_revenue_documented.xlsx' file must show the actual internal modifications (fixed errors, new labels, comments, or the new sheet).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original spreadsheet contains cryptic abbreviations (SR, GEN, STD, COMP, GP, OH, NP, BEP) and a deliberate #REF! error in row 6 column B. The agent must fix this error, expand/explain the abbreviations with descriptive labels, add cell comments, and create a 'Documentation' sheet explaining the logic.
